ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Continued To Apr-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Davod Rosenberg-Wohl, via ZOOM, for Kim Ridinger, via ZOOM, Personal Representative.

The court points counsel to the issues with the missing documents in this matter specifically regarding the Inventory and Appraisal ("I&A"). Counsel offers testimony regarding the required documents and that an I&A has been filed in a related matter. The court advises counsel that it is considering waiving the I&A in this matter. Counsel advises that an oversight caused the issue regarding notice to the heirs in Japan but is ready to serve them and file the proof of service within the next few days. The court confirms its tentative regarding waiving the I&A in this matter due to the unusual circumstance.

The Accounting Petition is continued to Apr-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Continued To Mar-08-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-26-2022 Continued To Feb-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
David Rosenberg-Wohl for and with Petitioner, Kim Ridinger.

The Court asked whether Mr. Ridinger has been acting as fiduciary without proper credentials as he is administrator in as least 2 other estates. Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl stated this Tanaka case needed to be set up in order to close the other case which is a relative of this person, that this is an extension of that case with three beneficiaries and one died, that this not a serial action, that there is no such thing as a personal representative in Japan, and that all beneficiaries live in Japan. The Court noted that the $2.7 million bond in Dornecker matter does not cover this matter, and asked counsel for his position on blocked accounts or bond for protection for the funds. Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl stated that they are not physically removing 1/3 from Dornecker into this Tanaka matter, that the funds are to be distributed not managed in Tanaka and that he would like to be able to report on 12/16/2020 hearing in Dornecker about a place for funds for this case. The Court stated that Mr. Ridinger is a personal representative or unlicensed fiduciary in at least one other unrelated case. Mr. Ridinger stated that he isn't sure but he thought all other cases were closed except for one successor trustee case, that he is a retired after 41 years as a licensed tax preparer, that his clients have become like friends, that he has a hard time saying no when they as him to take care of their financial affairs after they pass, that he doesn't do it as a living and would prefer not to do it at all. The Court stated it could grant the petition, waiving defective publication and waiving bond if funds are placed in blocked accounts. Mr. Ridinger asked if it would be faster to have a bond as the people in Japan are anxious to have their money as soon as possible.

The Court stated the bond is set at $850,000.00. Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl stated they can set that up within a day or so and submit to the court. Status on close of estate is set for March 14, 2022. Shirley Yawitz is appointed probate referee. Order appointing Kim Ridinger as Administrator is signed.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
